items.es: It is possible.
It is important.
It is not possible.
It is very important for me.

items.en: "Es posible, pero no es importante para mí."
items.es: It is possible, but it is not important for me.
I want to speak.
I want to eat.
I want to speak English.
I want to speak English with you.

items.en: "Quiero hablar inglés, pero no quiero comer ahora."
items.es: I want to speak English, but I don't want to eat now.
I can speak English.
Can I eat now?
I can do it.
Can I do it now?

items.en: "¿Puedo hacerlo ahora, o no es posible?"
items.es: Can I do it now, or is it not possible?
I have to work.
I have to go now.
I want a coffee.
items.note: En inglés "un tinto" se dice simplemente "a coffee".

items.en: "Quiero un café, pero tengo que trabajar."
items.es: I want a coffee, but I have to work.

items.en: "No puedo hablar ahora porque tengo que trabajar."
items.es: I can't speak now because I have to work.
I'm going to speak English.
I'm going to eat now.
Tomorrow I'm going to work.

items.en: "Hoy tengo que trabajar, pero mañana voy a hablar inglés contigo."
items.es: Today I have to work, but tomorrow I'm going to speak English with you.
Do you want to eat now?
Can you speak English?
Do you want a coffee?
Do you want to speak English with me tomorrow?
I want a coffee with you today.
Can you speak English now?
I'm going to speak English tomorrow.

items.en: "¿Quieres hablar inglés conmigo mañana, o no es posible?"
items.es: Do you want to speak English with me tomorrow, or is it not possible?
I need to buy.
items.note: En inglés SIEMPRE va el sujeto: I need (yo necesito). No puedes decir solo Need to.

items.es: I need to learn a little.
items.note: a little = un poco. Va después del verbo.

items.es: I need to practice English with you.
I want to see, but I need to work today.

items.en: "Necesito practicar un poco hoy, porque quiero aprender inglés."
items.es: I need to practice a little today, because I want to learn English.
I like to speak.
items.note: I like to + verbo = me gusta + verbo. En inglés el sujeto I es obligatorio.

items.es: I like to speak English.
I also like to eat.
items.note: also (también) va entre el sujeto y el verbo: I also like.

items.es: I don't like to work a lot.
items.note: Para negar usamos don't: I don't like. a lot = mucho.

items.en: "Me gusta mucho practicar inglés contigo, pero no me gusta trabajar hoy."
items.es: I like to practice English with you a lot, but I don't like to work today.
I want something.
items.note: something = algo. nothing = nada. everything = todo.

items.es: I need something for you.
items.note: for you = para ti. for me = para mí.

items.es: I want nothing today.
items.note: nothing ya es negativo, por eso decimos I want nothing (no I don't want nothing).

items.es: I can do everything for you.

items.en: "Quiero hacer algo para ti hoy, pero necesito practicar un poco."
items.es: I want to do something for you today, but I need to practice a little.
He wants to work.
items.note: En inglés SIEMPRE va el pronombre (he). Con he/she el verbo lleva -s: he wants.

items.es: She wants to eat now.
We can do it.
items.note: we = nosotros, they = ellos. Con I/you/we/they el verbo NO cambia: we can, they want.

items.es: They want to learn English.

items.en: "Ella quiere hablar contigo, pero nosotros necesitamos trabajar hoy."
items.es: She wants to speak with you, but we need to work today.
I want a coffee.
I can do it today.
I have to work tomorrow.
I'm going to learn English with you.

items.en: "Hoy necesito practicar un poco, porque mañana quiero hablar inglés contigo, pero no me gusta trabajar mucho."
items.es: Today I need to practice a little, because tomorrow I want to speak English with you, but I don't like to work a lot.
I am tired.
items.note: El verbo 'to be' cambia según quién: I am, you are, he/she/it is, we/they are. En inglés SIEMPRE necesitas el pronombre (no se dice solo 'soy', sino 'I am').

items.es: She is happy.
We are tired today.

items.en: "Estoy feliz, pero estoy un poco cansado."
items.es: I am happy, but I am a little tired.

items.en: "Ella está feliz hoy porque tú estás aquí con ella."
items.es: She is happy today because you are here with her.
I work here.
items.note: En tercera persona (he/she/it) el verbo lleva una -s: I work / he works, I live / she lives. Solo en he/she/it.

items.es: He works a lot.
She lives here.

items.en: "Yo vivo aquí, pero él trabaja allá."
items.es: I live here, but he works there.

items.en: "Ella vive aquí y trabaja mucho todos los días."
items.es: She lives here and works a lot every day.
Do you work?
items.note: En inglés las preguntas se forman con 'do' (I/you/we/they) o 'does' (he/she/it). Con 'does' el verbo principal pierde la -s: Does she live?

items.es: Does she live here?
Does he work a lot?
Do you want to practice English with me today?

items.en: "¿Ella trabaja aquí todos los días o solo hoy?"
items.es: Does she work here every day or only today?
I don't work here.
items.note: Para negar usas 'don't' (I/you/we/they) o 'doesn't' (he/she/it). Con 'doesn't' el verbo pierde la -s: she doesn't eat.

items.es: She doesn't eat meat.
He doesn't live here.

items.en: "Yo no quiero comer ahora porque no tengo hambre."
items.es: I don't want to eat now because I am not hungry.

items.en: "Ella no come carne, pero come pescado todos los días."
items.es: She doesn't eat meat, but she eats fish every day.
Where do you live?
items.note: Las palabras de pregunta van antes de do/does: what, where, how, when, who, why. Ej: Where do you live? What does he want?

items.es: What does he want?
When do you work?
Why doesn't she eat meat?

items.en: "¿Dónde trabaja él y qué quiere hacer hoy?"
items.es: Where does he work and what does he want to do today?
I want a coffee.
items.note: 'a' antes de consonante (a coffee), 'an' antes de sonido de vocal (an apple). 'the' es específico, lo conocido (the house).

items.es: I want an apple.
The house is here.

items.en: "Quiero comprar una manzana y un café hoy."
items.es: I want to buy an apple and a coffee today.

items.en: "La casa está aquí, pero quiero comprar un café también."
items.es: The house is here, but I want to buy a coffee also.
I want this.
items.note: this = esto/esta (cerca, singular), that = eso/esa (lejos, singular), these = estos (cerca, plural), those = esos (lejos, plural).

items.es: That is for you.
These are for me.
That house is very big.

items.en: "Esto es para ti y esos son para mí también."
items.es: This is for you and those are for me also.
The houses are here.
items.note: El plural normal añade -s o -es (house/houses, box/boxes). Pero hay irregulares: man/men, woman/women, child/children.

items.es: The men work here.
The women live here.
The children want to eat now.

items.en: "Las mujeres y los niños viven aquí, pero los hombres trabajan allá."
items.es: The women and the children live here, but the men work there.
I have a car.
items.note: 'have' para I/you/we/they, 'has' para he/she/it (igual que la regla de la -s): I have a car, she has a dog.

items.es: She has a dog.
We have a house.

items.en: "Él tiene un carro, pero no tiene una casa."
items.es: He has a car, but he doesn't have a house.

items.en: "Yo tengo un perro y ella tiene un carro muy grande también."
items.es: I have a dog and she has a very big car also.
There is a coffee here.
items.note: 'There is' para singular (hay un...), 'there are' para plural (hay varios...). Pregunta: Is there...? Negativo: There aren't...

items.es: There are a lot of houses here.
Is there a dog here?
There aren't children here today.

items.en: "Hay un café aquí, pero no hay manzanas hoy."
items.es: There is a coffee here, but there aren't apples today.
My friend is here.
items.note: Posesivos: my, your, his, her, our, their (van antes del sustantivo). Para nombres usa 's: Maria's house = la casa de Maria.

items.es: Your house is very big.
Her dog is here.

items.en: "La casa de Maria es muy grande."
items.es: Maria's house is very big.

items.en: "Mi amigo tiene un perro y su casa (de él) está aquí también."
items.es: My friend has a dog and his house is here also.
I am working now.
items.note: Para algo que pasa AHORA usa am/is/are + verbo-ing: I am working now. Para hábitos (todos los días) usa el presente simple: I work every day.

items.es: She is eating now.
We are learning English.

items.en: "Yo trabajo todos los días, pero ahora estoy comiendo."
items.es: I work every day, but now I am eating.

items.en: "Ella está aprendiendo inglés ahora porque quiere trabajar aquí."
items.es: She is learning English now because she wants to work here.
A big house.
items.note: Los adjetivos NUNCA cambian (no tienen plural ni género) y van ANTES del sustantivo: a big house, two big houses.

items.es: Two big houses.
She has a big dog.
I want a big house here.

items.en: "Mi amigo tiene una casa grande y dos perros grandes también."
items.es: My friend has a big house and two big dogs also.
I see him.
items.note: Pronombres de objeto (reciben la acción): me, you, him, her, us, them. Van después del verbo: I see him, she helps us.

items.es: She helps us.

items.en: "Yo la veo (a ella) todos los días."
items.es: I see her every day.
I want to see you today.

items.en: "Ella nos ayuda mucho porque yo no los veo (a ellos) todos los días."
items.es: She helps us a lot because I don't see them every day.
I am in the house.
items.note: in (en, dentro), on (sobre, en un día), at (en un lugar/hora), with (con), for (para), to (a, hacia). Cada una tiene sus usos.

items.es: I want to go to the house.
This is for you.
I want to work with you today.

items.en: "Estoy en la casa ahora, pero quiero ir contigo a trabajar."
items.es: I am in the house now, but I want to go with you to work.
I always work.
items.note: Adverbios de frecuencia (always, usually, sometimes, never) van ANTES del verbo principal: I always work; she never eats here.

items.es: She never eats here.
I usually work here.
Sometimes I want to eat fish.

items.en: "Yo siempre trabajo aquí, pero ella nunca come con nosotros."
items.es: I always work here, but she never eats with us.
I can speak English.
items.note: 'can' = poder (habilidad): I can speak English. 'have to' / 'must' = tener que (obligación): I have to work today.

items.es: I have to work today.
I must go now.

items.en: "Puedo ayudarte, pero tengo que trabajar hoy."
items.es: I can help you, but I have to work today.

items.en: "Ella puede hablar inglés, pero hoy tiene que trabajar con nosotros."
items.es: She can speak English, but today she has to work with us.
I am happy today.
items.note: Repaso de la Etapa 2: descríbete a ti mismo, dónde vives, tu rutina y qué estás haciendo ahora. Sin gramática nueva.

items.en: "Yo vivo aquí y trabajo todos los días."
items.es: I live here and I work every day.

items.en: "Tengo un perro grande y una casa pequeña."
items.es: I have a big dog and a small house.

items.en: "Ahora estoy aprendiendo inglés porque quiero hablar contigo."
items.es: Now I am learning English because I want to speak with you.

items.en: "Me llamo Maria, vivo aquí con mi amigo, siempre trabajo mucho, y ahora estoy aprendiendo inglés porque quiero hablarlo todos los días."
items.es: My name is Maria, I live here with my friend, I always work a lot, and now I am learning English because I want to speak it every day.
I want to practice because I like it.
items.note: "so" = "así que" (resultado). "also" normalmente va antes del verbo principal.

items.es: I speak English and Spanish.
I need to work, but I am tired.
Do you want coffee or tea?

items.en: "Estoy cansado, así que voy a casa."
items.es: I am tired, so I am going home.

items.en: "Estudio inglés porque quiero trabajar aquí, y también me gusta."
items.es: I study English because I want to work here, and I also like it.
I think that it is good.
items.note: En inglés "that" (que) se puede omitir, pero aquí lo usamos para aprenderlo: "I think (that) it is good".

items.es: I know that you speak English.
She thinks that I am busy.
I think that this food is good.
We know that they live here.

items.en: "Creo que él quiere ayudarnos porque es muy amable."
items.es: I think that he wants to help us because he is very kind.
If you want, we can go.
items.note: Con "if" + presente, la otra parte usa presente o "can". Recuerda: en 3a persona el verbo lleva -s (she studies).

items.es: If you have time, help me.
If she studies, she learns.
If you are hungry, we can eat.
If you don't work, you don't have money.

items.en: "Si quieres practicar inglés, puedo ayudarte porque tengo tiempo."
items.es: If you want to practice English, I can help you because I have time.
When I work, I listen to music.
items.note: "while" = mientras (al mismo tiempo). "although" = aunque (contraste). "when" = cuando.

items.es: While I eat, I read.
Although I am tired, I want to go.
When she speaks, I listen.
Although it is expensive, I want it.

items.en: "Mientras trabajo escucho música, aunque a veces necesito silencio."
items.es: While I work I listen to music, although sometimes I need silence.
I want some coffee.
items.note: Usa "some" en frases afirmativas; usa "any" en preguntas y negativas.

items.es: I don't have any money.
Is there any food?
I have some friends here.
Do you have any time for me?

items.en: "Quiero algo de agua porque tengo sed, pero no tengo dinero."
items.es: I want some water because I am thirsty, but I don't have any money.

t: much / many / a lot of / a few

items.es: How much money do you have?
items.note: "much" para incontables (time, money); "many" para contables (friends). "a lot of" sirve para ambos. "a few" = unos pocos (contable); "a little" = un poco (incontable).

items.es: How many friends do you have?
I have a lot of books.
I don't have much time.
I have a few friends here.

items.en: "No tengo mucho dinero, pero tengo muchos amigos y un poco de tiempo."
items.es: I don't have much money, but I have a lot of friends and a little time.
This house is bigger.
items.note: Adjetivos cortos: añade -er (big→bigger, tall→taller). Adjetivos largos: usa "more" (more expensive). "than" = que. Irregular: good→better (mejor).

items.es: I am taller than you.

items.en: "Este libro es más interesante que ese."
items.es: This book is more interesting than that one.

items.en: "Mi carro es más caro que tu carro."
items.es: My car is more expensive than your car.
She is faster than him.

items.en: "Creo que este restaurante es más caro que ese, pero la comida es mejor."
items.es: I think that this restaurant is more expensive than that one, but the food is better.
It is the biggest house.
items.note: Adjetivos cortos: the + -est (biggest). Adjetivos largos: the most (the most important). Irregular: good→the best (el mejor).

items.es: I am the tallest.
This is the most important thing.

items.en: "Es el restaurante más caro de la ciudad."
items.es: It is the most expensive restaurant in the city.

items.en: "Ella es la persona más amable aquí."
items.es: She is the kindest person here.

items.en: "Creo que este es el libro más interesante, y también el más caro."
items.es: I think that this is the most interesting book, and also the most expensive.
Could you help me?
items.note: "could" es más suave/cortés que "can". "would like" = quisiera (más educado que "want"). Tras "would like" + sustantivo o + to + verbo (I would like to eat).

items.es: I would like a coffee.
Could I speak with you?
She would like some water.
Could you speak more slowly, please?

items.en: "Quisiera practicar inglés contigo, así que ¿podrías ayudarme mañana?"
items.es: I would like to practice English with you, so could you help me tomorrow?
I am going to travel tomorrow.
items.note: "going to" + verbo = planes futuros. Usa am/is/are según la persona: I am going to, she is going to, we are going to.

items.es: What are you going to do?
She is going to study English.
We are going to eat now.
I am not going to work tomorrow.

items.en: "Mañana voy a estudiar inglés porque quiero hablar mejor."
items.es: Tomorrow I am going to study English because I want to speak better.

t: Time and days: at / on / in

items.es: It is three o'clock.
items.note: Hora exacta: "at" (at nine). Días: "on" (on Monday). Partes del día: "in" (in the morning). "o'clock" solo con horas en punto.

items.es: I work at nine.
I see you on Monday.
I study in the morning.
What time is it?

items.en: "El lunes en la mañana voy a trabajar a las nueve."
items.es: On Monday morning I am going to work at nine.
I'd like a coffee, please.
items.note: "I'd like" = "I would like" (forma corta). "Can I have...?" es muy común al pedir. "the check" (US) = "the bill" (UK) = la cuenta.

items.es: Can I have the check, please?
A table for two, please.
Can I have some water?
I'd like to see the menu.

items.en: "Una mesa para dos, por favor, y quisiera ver el menú."
items.es: A table for two, please, and I'd like to see the menu.
Turn right.
items.note: "turn" = girar; "go straight" = seguir derecho. "near" = cerca, "far" = lejos. "block" = cuadra.

items.es: Turn left.
Go straight.
It is near here.
It is two blocks from here.

items.en: "Sigue derecho, gira a la derecha, y el restaurante está cerca, a dos cuadras."
items.es: Go straight, turn right, and the restaurant is near, two blocks from here.
How much is it?
items.note: "How much is it?" = ¿cuánto cuesta? "expensive" = caro, "cheap" = barato. Recuerda el comparativo: cheaper = más barato.

items.es: It is very expensive.
It is cheap.
It is twenty dollars.
Can I pay with a card?

items.en: "¿Cuánto cuesta? Es muy caro, así que quisiera algo más barato."
items.es: How much is it? It is very expensive, so I'd like something cheaper.
I like cooking.
items.note: Tras like/love/enjoy se usa el verbo con -ing (cooking, reading). En 3a persona el primer verbo lleva -s: she likes singing.

items.es: I love reading.
I enjoy learning English.
She likes singing.
We enjoy working together.

items.en: "Me encanta cocinar y también disfruto aprender inglés porque es divertido."
items.es: I love cooking and I also enjoy learning English because it is fun.
I want you to help me.
items.note: Estructura: want/need + persona (object pronoun) + to + verbo. Usa los pronombres objeto: me, you, him, her, us, them. Ej: I want HIM to come.

items.es: I need him to come.

items.en: "Ella quiere que tú hables con ella."
items.es: She wants you to speak with her.
We need them to work today.
I want her to practice with me.

items.en: "Quiero que me ayudes mañana porque necesito que tú hables con ellos."
items.es: I want you to help me tomorrow because I need you to speak with them.
Look.
items.note: El imperativo usa el verbo sin sujeto (Look, Wait). Para negarlo: "Don't" + verbo (Don't worry = no te preocupes).

items.es: Wait.
Come here.
Please sit down.
Don't worry.

items.en: "Por favor, siéntate aquí y espera, no te preocupes."
items.es: Please, sit down here and wait, don't worry.
Excuse me, how much is this book?
items.note: Checkpoint: combina todo lo de la Etapa 3 (conectores, if, comparativos, going to, cortesía, direcciones, compras). Sin gramática nueva.

items.en: "Es caro, así que quisiera algo más barato."
items.es: It is expensive, so I'd like something cheaper.
Can I pay with a card, please?
Could you tell me where the restaurant is?

items.en: "Sigue derecho y gira a la derecha; está cerca."
items.es: Go straight and turn right; it is near.

items.en: "Quisiera una mesa para dos a las ocho, y si tienes tiempo, podrías ayudarme con el menú porque mi inglés no es muy bueno."
items.es: I'd like a table for two at eight, and if you have time, could you help me with the menu because my English is not very good.
I was tired.
She was happy yesterday.
They were here.
We were at the restaurant.

items.en: "Yo estaba muy cansado porque trabajé mucho, pero ella estaba feliz."
items.es: I was very tired because I worked a lot, but she was happy.
I worked yesterday.
items.note: El pasado regular se forma con -ed: work→worked.

items.es: She lived in Spain.
I studied English.
items.note: Verbos en -y cambian a -ied: study→studied.

items.es: We watched the movie and we enjoyed it.

items.en: "Trabajé mucho y estudié inglés porque quiero hablar contigo."
items.es: I worked a lot and I studied English because I want to speak with you.

t: Irregular past 1: went, ate, had, did, made

items.es: I went to the store.
items.note: go→went es irregular; hay que memorizarlo.

items.es: She ate pizza.
items.note: eat→ate.

items.es: We had a party.
items.note: have→had.

items.es: I did my homework.
items.note: do→did.

items.en: "Fui al restaurante, comí pizza e hice mi tarea."
items.es: I went to the restaurant, I ate pizza and I did my homework.

t: Irregular past 2: saw, came, said, got, took

items.es: I saw your brother.
items.note: see→saw.

items.es: She came to my house.
items.note: come→came.

items.es: He told the truth.
items.note: "tell the truth" usa tell→told.

items.es: I took a taxi.
items.note: take→took.

items.en: "Vi a tu hermano cuando vino a mi casa y dijo la verdad."
items.es: I saw your brother when he came to my house and he told the truth.
I didn't go.
items.note: En pasado, did + verbo base (sin -ed): didn't go, no didn't went.

items.es: Did you eat?
items.note: Pregunta: Did + sujeto + verbo base.

items.es: She didn't work yesterday.
Did you study English?

items.en: "No fui a la fiesta porque trabajé, pero ¿comiste algo allí?"
items.es: I didn't go to the party because I worked, but did you eat something there?
I saw her yesterday.
I worked a lot last week.
We went to Spain last year.
He came two days ago.
items.note: "ago" = hace; va después del tiempo: two days ago.

items.en: "Anoche comí en un restaurante y ayer vi a tu hermano."
items.es: Last night I ate at a restaurant and yesterday I saw your brother.

t: Telling a story: first, then, finally

items.es: First, I went to the store.
Then, I bought the food.
After that, I cooked dinner.
Finally, we ate together.

items.en: "Primero fui a la tienda, luego compré la comida y finalmente cociné la cena para mi familia."
items.es: First I went to the store, then I bought the food and finally I cooked dinner for my family.
I was working.
items.note: Pasado continuo: was/were + verbo-ing (acción en progreso).

items.es: She was cooking.
They were eating.
We were studying English.

items.en: "Yo estaba trabajando mientras ella estaba cocinando la cena."
items.es: I was working while she was cooking dinner.
I was eating when she called.
items.note: Continuo = acción en progreso; simple = acción que la interrumpe.

items.es: He arrived while I was cooking.
We were sleeping when you arrived.
She was studying when I saw her.

items.en: "Yo estaba trabajando cuando mi hermano llamó, así que no comí."
items.es: I was working when my brother called, so I didn't eat.
I have lived here.
items.note: Presente perfecto: have/has + participio; para experiencias de vida.

items.es: She has worked here.
Have you ever eaten sushi?
items.note: "Have you ever...?" = ¿alguna vez has...?

items.es: We have studied a lot.

items.en: "He vivido aquí por mucho tiempo, pero ¿alguna vez has viajado a España?"
items.es: I have lived here for a long time, but have you ever traveled to Spain?
I have seen that movie.
items.note: see→seen (participio irregular).

items.es: I have done my homework.
items.note: do→done.

items.es: I have been to Mexico.
items.note: be→been; go→gone.

items.es: She has written a book.
items.note: write→written; eat→eaten.

items.en: "He estado en México y he comido comida mexicana, pero nunca he escrito un libro."
items.es: I have been to Mexico and I have eaten Mexican food, but I have never written a book.

items.en: "El fin de semana pasado fui a la playa."
items.es: Last weekend I went to the beach.
It was raining when we arrived.

items.en: "Comimos en un restaurante y bebimos café."
items.es: We ate at a restaurant and we drank coffee.

items.en: "Mientras ella leía, yo estaba escuchando música."
items.es: While she was reading, I was listening to music.

items.en: "El fin de semana pasado fui a la playa con mi familia, estaba lloviendo cuando llegamos, así que comimos en un restaurante."
items.es: Last weekend I went to the beach with my family, it was raining when we arrived, so we ate at a restaurant.

t: Perfect vs simple: already, yet, just

items.es: I have just eaten.
items.note: "just" = acabar de; con presente perfecto.

items.es: I have already done my homework.
items.note: "already" = ya (afirmativo).

items.es: Have you eaten yet?
items.note: "yet" = todavía/ya; en preguntas y negativos, al final.

items.es: Did you go to the store yesterday?
items.note: Con tiempo terminado (yesterday) se usa pasado simple, no perfecto.

items.en: "Ya he hecho mi tarea y acabo de comer, pero ¿fuiste a la tienda ayer?"
items.es: I have already done my homework and I have just eaten, but did you go to the store yesterday?
I have lived here for two years.
items.note: "for" = por (duración); "since" = desde (punto de inicio).

items.es: I have worked here since 2020.

items.en: "Ella ha estudiado inglés por tres meses."
items.es: She has studied English for three months.
We have been friends since school.

items.en: "He vivido aquí por dos años y he trabajado en esta tienda desde el año pasado."
items.es: I have lived here for two years and I have worked in this store since last year.
I used to live there.
items.note: "used to" = solía/antes (hábito del pasado que ya no ocurre).

items.es: I used to smoke.
She used to work here.
We used to eat together.

items.en: "Yo solía vivir en España y solía trabajar en un restaurante, pero ahora vivo aquí."
items.es: I used to live in Spain and I used to work in a restaurant, but now I live here.
I saw him yesterday.
She told me.
items.note: tell→told; "me dijo" = told me.

items.es: We helped them.
He called you last night.

items.en: "La vi ayer y ella me dijo que vino a tu casa."
items.es: I saw her yesterday and she told me that she came to your house.
When I arrived, they were eating.

items.en: "Estaba cansado, así que me fui a casa."
items.es: I was tired, so I went home.
I studied a lot, but I didn't pass.

items.en: "Mientras ella cocinaba, yo limpiaba la casa."
items.es: While she was cooking, I was cleaning the house.

items.en: "Cuando llegué a casa, mi familia estaba comiendo, así que comí con ellos."
items.es: When I arrived home, my family was eating, so I ate with them.
Last year I traveled to Mexico.
I have never eaten such good food.

items.en: "Mientras caminaba por la ciudad, vi muchas cosas hermosas."
items.es: While I was walking through the city, I saw many beautiful things.

items.en: "He estado allí tres veces y siempre lo disfruto."
items.es: I have been there three times and I always enjoy it.

items.en: "El año pasado viajé a México con mi familia, mientras caminábamos por la ciudad vimos muchas cosas hermosas, comimos comida deliciosa, y he querido volver desde entonces."
items.es: Last year I traveled to Mexico with my family, while we were walking through the city we saw many beautiful things, we ate delicious food, and I have wanted to go back since then.
I will call you.
items.note: "will" + verbo base (sin "to"): will call, will help, will go.

items.es: She will help us.
I will call you tomorrow.
I think he will come later.

items.en: "No te preocupes, te ayudaré con eso."
items.es: Don't worry, I will help you with that.

items.en: "Voy a visitar a mi familia este fin de semana."
items.es: I am going to visit my family this weekend.
items.note: "going to" = plan que ya tenías. "will" = decisión espontánea o predicción.

items.es: Okay, I will do it now.
items.note: Decisión espontánea en el momento: "will".

items.es: I think it will rain tomorrow.
items.note: Predicción sin plan: "will".

items.en: "Vamos a abrir un restaurante el próximo año."
items.es: We are going to open a restaurant next year.

items.en: "No tengo planes, pero creo que me quedaré en casa."
items.es: I don't have plans, but I think I will stay home.
If it rains, I will stay home.
items.note: Primer condicional: If + presente simple, … will + verbo base. Situación real y posible.

items.es: If you have time, I will help you.
If you study, you will pass the exam.
If she comes, we will eat together.

items.en: "Si terminas tu trabajo temprano, iremos al cine."
items.es: If you finish your work early, we will go to the movies.
I would like a coffee, please.
items.note: "would like" = me gustaría (más cortés que "want").

items.es: Would you like to come with me?
I would love to visit Italy.
Would you like something to eat?

items.en: "Me encantaría ayudarte, pero estoy muy ocupado hoy."
items.es: I would love to help you, but I am very busy today.
If I had money, I would travel.
items.note: Segundo condicional: If + pasado simple, … would + verbo base. Situación imaginaria (no real ahora).

items.en: "Si fuera rico, compraría una casa grande."
items.es: If I were rich, I would buy a big house.
items.note: En inglés se usa "were" para todas las personas en lo imaginario: If I were…

items.en: "Si tuviera más tiempo, aprendería a cocinar."
items.es: If I had more time, I would learn to cook.

items.en: "Si viviera en la playa, nadaría todos los días."
items.es: If I lived on the beach, I would swim every day.

items.en: "Si pudiera hablar inglés perfectamente, trabajaría en otro país."
items.es: If I could speak English perfectly, I would work in another country.
You should rest.
items.note: "should" = debería. Para dar consejos. Va con verbo base, sin "to".

items.es: I should go now.
You should drink more water.
You shouldn't work so much.
items.note: Negativo: should not / shouldn't.

items.en: "Si estás cansado, deberías dormir más temprano esta noche."
items.es: If you are tired, you should sleep earlier tonight.
It might rain later.
items.note: "might" = quizás / es posible. "could" = podría. Ambos van con verbo base.

items.es: I could be wrong.
She might come tomorrow.

items.en: "Podríamos ir a la playa si hace buen tiempo."
items.es: We could go to the beach if the weather is good.

items.en: "No estoy seguro, pero creo que él podría llegar tarde."
items.es: I am not sure, but I think he might be late.

t: must / have to / don't have to

items.es: You must stop.
items.note: "must" = obligación fuerte. "have to" = tener que. "don't have to" = NO es obligatorio (no que esté prohibido).

items.es: I have to work.
You don't have to come.
items.note: "don't have to" = no es obligatorio. ¡Ojo! No significa "no debes".

items.en: "Ella tiene que estudiar para el examen."
items.es: She has to study for the exam.

items.en: "No tienes que pagar ahora, pero debes hacerlo antes del viernes."
items.es: You don't have to pay now, but you must do it before Friday.
I have been working all day.
items.note: have/has + been + verbo-ing. Acción que empezó antes y sigue ahora (o acaba de terminar).

items.es: She has been studying English.
We have been waiting for an hour.
What have you been doing lately?

items.en: "He estado aprendiendo inglés desde enero y ya puedo entender mucho."
items.es: I have been learning English since January and I can already understand a lot.
English is spoken here.
items.note: Pasiva: is/was + participio. El foco está en la acción, no en quién la hace.

items.es: It was made in Colombia.

items.en: "El café se cultiva en las montañas."
items.es: Coffee is grown in the mountains.

items.en: "La casa fue construida el año pasado."
items.es: The house was built last year.

items.en: "Este libro fue escrito por un autor famoso hace muchos años."
items.es: This book was written by a famous author many years ago.
He said that he was tired.
items.note: En estilo indirecto el verbo suele cambiar al pasado: is → was, will → would.

items.es: She told me that she would come.
items.note: "told" lleva persona: told me. "said" normalmente no: said that…

items.es: He said that he was busy.

items.en: "Ella me dijo que le gustaba la película."
items.es: She told me that she liked the movie.

items.en: "Mi amigo me dijo que había estado trabajando mucho últimamente."
items.es: My friend told me that he had been working a lot lately.
I get up at six.
items.note: Phrasal verbs: verbo + partícula con significado propio. get up = levantarse.

items.es: Please turn on the light.
items.note: turn on = encender. turn off = apagar.

items.es: I am looking for my keys.
items.note: look for = buscar.

items.es: Don't give up.
items.note: give up = rendirse.

items.en: "Me levanté temprano y encendí la computadora para buscar trabajo."
items.es: I got up early and turned on the computer to look for work.
I found out yesterday.
items.note: find out = averiguar / enterarse.

items.es: We ran out of milk.
items.note: run out of = quedarse sin algo.

items.es: Don't put it off.
items.note: put off = posponer / aplazar.

items.en: "Me enteré de que la tienda estaba cerrada."
items.es: I found out that the store was closed.

items.en: "Nos quedamos sin tiempo, así que pospusimos la reunión hasta el lunes."
items.es: We ran out of time, so we put off the meeting until Monday.
I think you are right.
items.note: "I think" = creo / pienso. Para dar tu opinión.

items.en: "En mi opinión, es una buena idea."
items.es: In my opinion, it is a good idea.
I agree with you.
items.note: "agree" no lleva "to be": NO "I am agree", sino "I agree".

items.es: I don't agree.

items.en: "En mi opinión, deberíamos esperar, pero entiendo si no estás de acuerdo."
items.es: In my opinion, we should wait, but I understand if you don't agree.
Let's go out tonight.
items.note: "Let's" + verbo base = hagamos algo (sugerencia).

items.es: Why don't we eat something?
items.note: "Why don't we…?" + verbo base = ¿por qué no…? (sugerencia).

items.es: How about going out?
items.note: "How about" + verbo-ing = ¿qué tal si…?

items.en: "¿Qué tal si vamos a la playa este fin de semana?"
items.es: How about going to the beach this weekend?

items.en: "Estoy aburrido, así que salgamos a caminar o veamos una película."
items.es: I am bored, so let's go for a walk or watch a movie.
I will call you when I get home.

items.en: "Si tuviera más tiempo, viajaría por todo el mundo."
items.es: If I had more time, I would travel around the world.

items.en: "Creo que deberías descansar porque has estado trabajando mucho."
items.es: I think you should rest because you have been working a lot.

items.en: "¿Qué tal si salimos esta noche? Podría ser divertido."
items.es: How about going out tonight? It could be fun.

items.en: "En mi opinión, si estudias todos los días, aprenderás inglés rápido, pero no tienes que apurarte."
items.es: In my opinion, if you study every day, you will learn English fast, but you don't have to rush.
I'm gonna call you later.
items.note: "gonna" = "going to" en habla informal. Solo se escribe así en mensajes casuales, nunca en algo formal.

items.es: I wanna eat something.
items.note: "wanna" = "want to". Suena natural al hablar, pero evítalo en correos o textos serios.

items.es: I gotta go now.
items.note: "gotta" = "have got to / have to". Muy común al hablar; informal.

items.en: "¿Qué vas a hacer este fin de semana?"
items.es: What are you gonna do this weekend?
items.note: Otra vez "gonna" = "going to". Nota que en preguntas también es súper común.

items.en: "Voy a estar bien, solo me tengo que relajar."
items.es: I'm gonna be okay, I just gotta relax.
No worries.
items.note: "No worries" es la forma casual y relajada de decir "tranquilo / no pasa nada".

items.es: Never mind.
items.note: "Never mind" se usa cuando algo ya no importa o cuando quieres dejar un tema. Muy común al hablar.

items.es: It's up to you.
items.note: "It's up to you" = la decisión es tuya. Suena natural y amable.

items.es: I'm in.
items.note: "I'm in" significa que te apuntas o aceptas un plan. Informal y entusiasta.

items.en: "No te preocupes, tú decides y cuenta conmigo sea lo que sea."
items.es: No worries, it's up to you, and I'm in either way.
Well, I'm not so sure.
items.note: "Well…" al inicio da tiempo para pensar, como "bueno…" en español. Muletilla muy natural.

items.es: It's hard to explain, you know.
items.note: "you know" es una muletilla para conectar con quien te escucha, como "sabes" o "¿me entiendes?".

items.es: I mean, I didn't mean to.
items.note: "I mean" sirve para aclarar o corregir lo que dijiste, como "o sea…". "didn't mean to" = no fue mi intención.

items.es: Actually, I changed my mind.
items.note: "Actually" aquí introduce algo que corrige o sorprende, como "de hecho".

items.en: "Bueno, o sea, más o menos me gustó, sabes."
items.es: Well, I mean, I kind of liked it, you know.
items.note: "kind of" = más o menos / algo así, suaviza lo que dices.

items.en: "O sea, bueno, de hecho creo que esto va a salir increíble, sabes."
items.es: I mean, well, actually I think this is gonna turn out amazing, you know.
Could you help me with this, please?
items.note: "Could you…" es la forma cortés y formal de pedir algo. Ideal con desconocidos o en el trabajo.

items.es: Can you pass me the salt?
items.note: "Can you…" es más casual que "Could you…". Perfecto entre amigos o familia.

items.es: How's it going?
items.note: "How's it going?" es un saludo casual y amistoso, como "¿cómo te va?".

items.es: What's up?
items.note: "What's up?" es muy casual, entre amigos. Equivale al "¿qué más?" colombiano.

items.en: "Mucho gusto, ¿cómo le va?, ¿podría contarme un poco sobre usted?"
items.es: Nice to meet you, how's it going, and could you tell me a little about yourself?
Hey, what's up?
items.note: Saludo casual al contestar el teléfono o un mensaje. "Hey" es más relajado que "Hello".

items.es: Hold on.
items.note: "Hold on" = espera / un momento. Muy usado al teléfono cuando pides a alguien que aguante un segundo.

items.es: I'll text you.
items.note: "text" como verbo = mandar un mensaje. "I'll text you" = te escribo (por mensaje).

items.es: I'm on my way.
items.note: "I'm on my way" = voy en camino. Frase clave para avisar que ya saliste.

items.en: "Oye, ¿qué más?, espera, ya voy en camino y te escribo cuando llegue."
items.es: Hey, what's up, hold on, I'm on my way and I'll text you when I get there.
I'm so tired.
items.note: "so" aquí intensifica, como "muy". Natural y casual para hablar de cómo te sientes.

items.es: That's awesome!
items.note: "awesome" = genial / increíble. Una de las reacciones positivas más comunes en inglés casual.

items.es: No way!
items.note: "No way!" expresa sorpresa o incredulidad, como "¡no puede ser!".

items.es: I can't wait.
items.note: "I can't wait" = no puedo esperar, expresa emoción por algo que viene. Recuerda: "can't" = "cannot".

items.en: "Estoy agotado, pero esto es increíble y no puedo esperar a contarte."
items.es: I'm so tired, but this is awesome and I can't wait to tell you.

t: Real plan: the weekend with a friend

items.en: "Oye, ¿qué vas a hacer este fin de semana?"
items.es: Hey, what are you gonna do this weekend?
items.note: "gonna" = "going to". Apertura casual para proponer un plan.

items.es: I don't know, do you wanna grab something to eat?
items.note: "wanna" = "want to". "grab something to eat" = comer algo, muy natural entre amigos.

items.es: Sounds good, I'm in.
items.note: Repaso del Día 84: "sounds good" y "I'm in" para aceptar un plan con ganas.

items.en: "Pero me tengo que ir temprano, ¿está bien?"
items.es: But I gotta leave early, is that okay?
items.note: "gotta" = "have to". Aquí pones una condición amable al plan.

items.en: "Genial, entonces nos vemos el sábado, voy en camino apenas me escribas y no puedo esperar."
items.es: Awesome, so I'll see you on Saturday, I'm on my way as soon as you text me, and I can't wait.

items.en: "Hola, ¿cómo te va? Hace mucho que no hablamos."
items.es: Hey, how's it going? It's been a while since we talked.
items.note: Repaso de saludos casuales. "It's been a while" = ha pasado un tiempo, frase muy usada al reconectar.

items.en: "Estoy muy bien, de hecho tengo grandes noticias."
items.es: I'm so good, actually I have big news.
items.note: "so" intensifica y "actually" funciona como muletilla para introducir algo importante.

items.en: "¡No puede ser! Cuéntame, no puedo esperar."
items.es: No way! Tell me, I can't wait.
items.note: Reacciones del Día 88 juntas en una conversación real.

items.en: "Acabo de terminar mi curso de inglés y me siento increíble."
items.es: I just finished my English course and I feel awesome.
items.note: "just" + pasado = acabar de hacer algo. "awesome" = increíble.

items.en: "Gracias, sabes, fue mucho trabajo, pero ahora puedo hablar inglés con confianza, y esto es solo el comienzo, así que vamos a celebrar."
items.es: Thank you, you know, it was a lot of work, but now I can speak English with confidence, and this is just the beginning, so let's go celebrate.
